message:
perf(render): add span-aware tile diff builder; fix(runtime): guard reconcile_subscriptions behind self.running

## ftui-render: span-aware tile diff building

Add `build_from_buffer()` method to `TileDiffBuilder` that accepts a
`&Buffer` directly instead of the decomposed `TileDiffInput` struct.
This unlocks access to `Buffer::dirty_span_row()`, which provides
per-row dirty spans indicating which column ranges were actually
modified. The old `build()` method scanned every cell in a dirty row's
dirty_bits slice to accumulate tile counts; the new method narrows the
scan to only the column ranges covered by recorded dirty spans.

Key details:

- Extract `accumulate_tile_counts_range()` as a standalone helper that
  processes a single `Range<usize>` within a row's dirty_bits slice,
  mapping dirty cells to the correct tile columns and accumulating
  counts. Returns `Err(())` on u32 overflow for early bailout.

- `build_from_buffer()` iterates rows, queries `dirty_span_row(y)`,
  and dispatches: if spans are available and not marked full, iterate
  only the recorded spans; otherwise fall back to the full row range
  (0..width). This means screens with sparse mutations (e.g., a single
  cursor blink or spinner update) skip the vast majority of cells
  during SAT construction.

- The `scanned_cells` counter tracks exactly how many cells were
  visited, surfaced via `stats.sat_build_cells` for observability.

- `compute_dirty_changes()` call site updated to use
  `build_from_buffer(tile_config, new)` instead of manually
  destructuring the buffer into a `TileDiffInput`. This is a pure
  refactor of the call site with identical semantics.

- Add assertion to existing tile-diff-with-spans test verifying that
  `sat_build_cells == 3` (only the 3 explicitly dirtied cells are
  scanned, not the full row widths).

## ftui-runtime: guard reconcile_subscriptions against post-quit calls

Add `&& self.running` guard to two `reconcile_subscriptions()` call
sites in `program.rs`:

1. After screen-dispatch tick processing (~line 3864): previously, if
   the model's update handler issued a quit command during a screen
   tick, the program would still attempt to reconcile subscriptions on
   a model that may have already begun teardown.

2. After force-tick on screen transition (~line 4434): same scenario
   where a force-tick of the newly active screen could trigger a quit,
   and the subsequent reconcile would operate on stale/torn-down state.

Both guards mirror the existing pattern established in commit 6948470
("guard against post-quit subscription reconciliation") and prevent
potential panics or logic errors from reconciling subscriptions after
the program has already decided to stop running.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
